---
title: "SVMmodel"
author: "Mia Gale"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## SVM Model

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#install.packages("xgboost") #new pacakage
library(tidymodels)   # Core framework for modeling (includes recipes, workflows, parsnip, etc.)
library(finetune)     # Additional tuning strategies (e.g., racing, ANOVA-based tuning)
library(vip)          # For plotting variable importance from fitted models
library(xgboost)      # XGBoost implementation in R
library(ranger)       # Fast implementation of Random Forests
library(tidyverse)    # Data wrangling and visualization
library(doParallel)   # For parallel computing (useful during resampling/tuning)
library(caret)       # Other great library for Machine Learning 
install.packages("e1071")
library("e1071")
#install.packages("kernlab"")
#install.packages("kernlab")
library(kernlab)
```

Preprocess Training

```{r}
set.seed(9678965) # Setting seed to get reproducible results 

corn_split <- initial_split(corn_training_reduced,
                            prop = .7,
                            strata = yield_mg_ha) 
# proption of split same as previous codes
  
corn_split
```

```{r}
corn_train <- training(corn_split)  # 70% of data
corn_train #This is your traing data frame
```

```{r}
corn_test <- testing(corn_split)
corn_test
```

```{r}
svm_recipe <- recipe(yield_mg_ha ~ ., data = corn_train) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_center(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_scale(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  step_zv(all_predictors())
summary(svm_recipe)
```

Prepare Recipe

```{r}
svm_recipe_prepped <- prep(svm_recipe, training = corn_training_reduced)

svm_recipe_prepped
```

```{r}
Train_processed <- bake(svm_recipe_prepped, new_data = corn_train)
test_processed  <- bake(svm_recipe_prepped, new_data = corn_test)
```

Tuning Hyperparameters

```{r}
xgb_spec <- #Specifying XgBoost as our model type, asking to tune the hyperparameters
  boost_tree(
   # Total number of boosting iterations
    tree = tune(), 
         # Maximum depth of each tree
    tree_depth = tune(),
             # Minimum samples required to split a node
    min_n = tune(),
        # Step size shrinkage for each boosting step
    learn_rate = tune()
) %>%
        #specify engine 
  set_engine("xgboost") %>%
       # Set to mode
  set_mode("regression")

xgb_spec
```

```{r}

```
